heading: (intro)
raw: Directed by Frank Capra. Written by Robert Riskin, based on a story by Samuel Hopkins Adams.
clean: Directed by Frank Capra. Written by Robert Riskin, based on a story by Samuel Hopkins Adams.

heading: Peter Warne
raw: [to his boss, on the phone] In a pig's eye, you will!...Hey listen monkey face, when you fired me, you fired the best newshound your filthy scandal sheet ever had...That was free verse, you gashouse palooka!
clean: In a pig's eye, you will!...Hey listen monkey face, when you fired me, you fired the best newshound your filthy scandal sheet ever had...That was free verse, you gashouse palooka!

heading: Peter Warne
raw: [to his boss, who has already hung up] Oh, so you're changing your tune, eh? You're a little late with your apologies. I wouldn't go back to work for you if you begged me on your hands and knees. And I hope this will be a lesson to you.
clean: Oh, so you're changing your tune, eh? You're a little late with your apologies. I wouldn't go back to work for you if you begged me on your hands and knees. And I hope this will be a lesson to you.

heading: Peter Warne
raw: I never did like the idea of sitting on newspaper. I did it once, and all the headlines came off on my white pants. On the level! It actually happened. Nobody bought a paper that day. They just followed me around over town and read the news on the seat of my pants.
clean: I never did like the idea of sitting on newspaper. I did it once, and all the headlines came off on my white pants. On the level! It actually happened. Nobody bought a paper that day. They just followed me around over town and read the news on the seat of my pants.

heading: Peter Warne
raw: [to Ellie] Now listen, I put up a stiff fight for that seat. So if it's just the same to you - scram!
clean: Now listen, I put up a stiff fight for that seat. So if it's just the same to you - scram!

heading: Peter Warne
raw: [in a telegram] Am I laughing? The biggest scoop of the year just dropped in my lap. I know where Ellen Andrews is...How would you like to have the story, you big tub of mush...Will try and get it. What I said about never writing another line for you still goes. Are you burning? PETER WARNE
clean: Am I laughing? The biggest scoop of the year just dropped in my lap. I know where Ellen Andrews is...How would you like to have the story, you big tub of mush...Will try and get it. What I said about never writing another line for you still goes. Are you burning? PETER WARNE

heading: Peter Warne
raw: [to Ellie] Now that's my whole plot in a nutshell. A simple story for simple people. If you behave yourself, I'll see that you get to King Westley. If not, I'll just have to spill the beans to Papa.
clean: Now that's my whole plot in a nutshell. A simple story for simple people. If you behave yourself, I'll see that you get to King Westley. If not, I'll just have to spill the beans to Papa.

heading: Ellie Andrews
raw: [to Peter] There is a brain behind that face of yours, isn't there? You've got everything nicely figured out for yourself.
clean: There is a brain behind that face of yours, isn't there? You've got everything nicely figured out for yourself.

heading: Mr. Andrews
raw: [to King Westley] I admit I'm licked. But it's only because I'm worried. If I don't find her soon, I'll go crazy...if she returns, I won't interfere with your marriage.
clean: I admit I'm licked. But it's only because I'm worried. If I don't find her soon, I'll go crazy...if she returns, I won't interfere with your marriage.

heading: Others
raw: Detective: We're wasting our time. Can you imagine Ellie Andrews riding on a bus?
clean: We're wasting our time. Can you imagine Ellie Andrews riding on a bus?

heading: Others
raw: Shapeley: Shapeley's the name - and that's the way I like 'em!
clean: Shapeley's the name - and that's the way I like 'em!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Ellie: You've been telling me what not to do ever since I can remember.
clean: You've been telling me what not to do ever since I can remember.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mr. Andrews: That's because you've always been a stubborn idiot.
clean: That's because you've always been a stubborn idiot.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Ellie: I come from a long line of stubborn idiots.
clean: I come from a long line of stubborn idiots.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Ellie: I don't want it reported!...Can you understand English? Would you please keep out of my affairs. I want to be left alone.
clean: I don't want it reported!...Can you understand English? Would you please keep out of my affairs. I want to be left alone.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Peter: Why, you ungrateful brat!
clean: Why, you ungrateful brat!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Peter: Remember me? I'm the fellow you slept on last night.
clean: Remember me? I'm the fellow you slept on last night.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Ellie: You needn't concern yourself about me. I can take care of myself.
clean: You needn't concern yourself about me. I can take care of myself.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Peter: You'll never get away with it, Miss Andrews.
clean: You'll never get away with it, Miss Andrews.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Ellie: Listen, if you promise not to do it, I'll pay you. I'll pay you as much as he will. You won't gain anything by giving me away, as long I'm willing to make it worth your while. I've got to get to New York without being stopped. It's terribly important to me.

heading: Dialogue

heading: Dialogue

heading: Dialogue

heading: Dialogue

heading: Dialogue
raw: Peter: Hey now, wait a minute. Let's get this straightened out right now. If you're nursing any silly notion that I'm interested in you, forget it. You're just a headline to me.

heading: Dialogue

heading: Dialogue

heading: Dialogue
raw: Peter: Of course you shouldn't - you don't know how to do it. Dunking's an art. Don't let it soak so long. A dip and [he stuffs the donut in his mouth] plop, in your mouth. Let it hang there too long, it'll get soft and fall off. It's all a matter of timing. Aw, I oughta write a book about it.

heading: Dialogue

heading: Dialogue
raw: Peter: Supposin' I was to tell you that Ellen Andrews was going to have her marriage annulled...She's going to marry somebody else...Would a story like that would be worth a thousand bucks to you...I got it, Joe.
clean: Supposin' I was to tell you that Ellen Andrews was going to have her marriage annulled...She's going to marry somebody else...Would a story like that would be worth a thousand bucks to you...I got it, Joe.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Joe: Who's the guy she's gonna marry?
clean: Who's the guy she's gonna marry?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mr. Andrews: Now don't tell me you've fallen in love with a bus driver...Who is he?
clean: Now don't tell me you've fallen in love with a bus driver...Who is he?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Ellie: I don't know very much about him. Just that I love him.
clean: I don't know very much about him. Just that I love him.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mr. Andrews: Well, if it's as serious as all that, we'll move heaven and earth to...
clean: Well, if it's as serious as all that, we'll move heaven and earth to...

heading: Dialogue
raw: Ellie: No, it's no use. He despises me.
clean: No, it's no use. He despises me.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mr. Andrews: Oh, come now.
clean: Oh, come now.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Ellie: Yes he does. He despises everything about me. He says that I'm spoiled and selfish and pampered, and-and thoroughly insincere.
clean: Yes he does. He despises everything about me. He says that I'm spoiled and selfish and pampered, and-and thoroughly insincere.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mr. Andrews: Oh, ridiculous.
clean: Oh, ridiculous.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Ellie: He doesn't think so much of you either...He blames you for everything that's wrong with me. He says you raised me stupidly.
clean: He doesn't think so much of you either...He blames you for everything that's wrong with me. He says you raised me stupidly.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mr. Andrews: Now that's a fine man to fall in love with.
clean: Now that's a fine man to fall in love with.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Ellie: Oh, he's marvelous! I practically threw myself at him. I don't want to stir up any more trouble. I've done it all my life. I've made your life miserable and mine too. I'm tired Father. I'm tired of running around in circles...I've got to settle down. It doesn't matter how or where or with whom.
clean: Oh, he's marvelous! I practically threw myself at him. I don't want to stir up any more trouble. I've done it all my life. I've made your life miserable and mine too. I'm tired Father. I'm tired of running around in circles...I've got to settle down. It doesn't matter how or where or with whom.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mr. Andrews: Mr. Warne?
clean: Mr. Warne?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Peter: Yeah.
clean: Yeah.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mr. Andrews: Please sit down.
clean: Please sit down.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Peter: Thanks.
clean: Thanks.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mr. Andrews: I was surprised to get your note. My daughter hadn't told me anything about you, about your helping her.
clean: I was surprised to get your note. My daughter hadn't told me anything about you, about your helping her.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Peter: That's typical of your daughter. Take those things for granted. Why did you think I lugged her all the way from Miami - for the love of it?
clean: That's typical of your daughter. Take those things for granted. Why did you think I lugged her all the way from Miami - for the love of it?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mr. Andrews: She thinks you're entitled to anything you can get.
clean: She thinks you're entitled to anything you can get.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Peter: Oh she does, eh? Now isn't that sweet of her. You don't, I suppose.
clean: Oh she does, eh? Now isn't that sweet of her. You don't, I suppose.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mr. Andrews: I don't know. I'll have to see on what you base your claim. I presume you feel justified -
clean: I don't know. I'll have to see on what you base your claim. I presume you feel justified -

heading: Dialogue
raw: Peter: If I didn't, I wouldn't be here. [He pulls a list from his pocket] I've got it all itemized.
clean: If I didn't, I wouldn't be here. I've got it all itemized.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mr. Andrews: [Reading the list] 'Cash outlay, $8.60; topcoat, $15; suitcase, $7.50; hat, $4; three shirts, $4.50. Total, $39.60. All the above items had to be sold to buy gasoline.'
clean: Cash outlay, $8.60; topcoat, $15; suitcase, $7.50; hat, $4; three shirts, $4.50. Total, $39.60. All the above items had to be sold to buy gasoline.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Peter: And I sold some shorts and socks too. I'm throwing those in.
clean: And I sold some shorts and socks too. I'm throwing those in.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mr. Andrews: Yes, I know -
clean: Yes, I know -

heading: Dialogue
raw: Peter: What's the matter? Isn't it cheap enough? A trip like that would cost you a thousand dollars. Maybe more!
clean: What's the matter? Isn't it cheap enough? A trip like that would cost you a thousand dollars. Maybe more!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mr. Andrews: Now let me get this straight. You want $39.60 in addition to the $10,000?
clean: Now let me get this straight. You want $39.60 in addition to the $10,000?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Peter: What $10,000?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mr. Andrews: The reward.
clean: The reward.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Peter: Who said anything about a reward?
clean: Who said anything about a reward?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mr. Andrews: I'm afraid I'm a little bit confused. I assumed that you -
clean: I'm afraid I'm a little bit confused. I assumed that you -

heading: Dialogue
raw: Peter: Look, look, look, all I want is $39.60. And if you give me a check for it, I'll get outta this joint. It gives me the jitters.
clean: Look, look, look, all I want is $39.60. And if you give me a check for it, I'll get outta this joint. It gives me the jitters.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mr. Andrews: You're a peculiar chap.
clean: You're a peculiar chap.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Peter: Yeah, we'll go into that some other time.
clean: Yeah, we'll go into that some other time.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mr. Andrews: The average man would go after the reward. All you seem to -
clean: The average man would go after the reward. All you seem to -

heading: Dialogue
raw: Peter: Listen, did anybody ever make a sucker out of you? This is a matter of principle. Something you probably wouldn't understand. But when anybody takes me for a buggy ride, I don't like the idea of having to pay for the privilege.
clean: Listen, did anybody ever make a sucker out of you? This is a matter of principle. Something you probably wouldn't understand. But when anybody takes me for a buggy ride, I don't like the idea of having to pay for the privilege.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mr. Andrews: Were you taken for a buggy ride?
clean: Were you taken for a buggy ride?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Peter: Yes. With all the trimming. So how about the check? Do I get it?
clean: Yes. With all the trimming. So how about the check? Do I get it?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mr. Andrews: Certainly.
clean: Certainly.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Peter: Thanks.
clean: Thanks.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mr. Andrews: [Smiling, he writes a check] Here you are.
clean: Here you are.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Peter: Thank you.
clean: Thank you.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mr. Andrews: Oh, ah, do you mind if I ask you a question frankly? Do you love my daughter?
clean: Oh, ah, do you mind if I ask you a question frankly? Do you love my daughter?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Peter: Any guy that'd fall in love with your daughter ought to have his head examined.
clean: Any guy that'd fall in love with your daughter ought to have his head examined.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mr. Andrews: That's an evasion.
clean: That's an evasion.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Peter: She picked herself a perfect running mate: King Westley! The pill of the century! What she needs is a guy that'd take a sock at her once a day - whether it is coming to her or not. If you had half the brains you're supposed to have, you'd have done it yourself long ago.
clean: She picked herself a perfect running mate: King Westley! The pill of the century! What she needs is a guy that'd take a sock at her once a day - whether it is coming to her or not. If you had half the brains you're supposed to have, you'd have done it yourself long ago.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mr. Andrews: Do you love her?
clean: Do you love her?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Peter: A normal human being couldn't live under the same roof with her without going nutty. She's my idea of nothing!
clean: A normal human being couldn't live under the same roof with her without going nutty. She's my idea of nothing!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mr. Andrews: I asked you a simple question! Do you love her?
clean: I asked you a simple question! Do you love her?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Peter: [As he departs and slams the office door] Yes! But don't hold that against me. I'm a little screwy myself.
clean: Yes! But don't hold that against me. I'm a little screwy myself.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Ellie: Well, here's to the merry go round.
clean: Well, here's to the merry go round.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Peter: Perfect. Now you look natural.
clean: Perfect. Now you look natural.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Ellie: I hope you got your money.
clean: I hope you got your money.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Peter: You bet I did.
clean: You bet I did.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Ellie: Congratulations.
clean: Congratulations.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Peter: Thanks, same to you.
clean: Thanks, same to you.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Ellie: Stay around and watch the fun. You'll enjoy it immensely.
clean: Stay around and watch the fun. You'll enjoy it immensely.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Peter: I would, but I've got a weak stomach.
clean: I would, but I've got a weak stomach.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Peter: [in a telegram] What's holding up the annulment, you slowpoke? The walls of Jericho are a-toppling.
clean: What's holding up the annulment, you slowpoke? The walls of Jericho are a-toppling.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mr. Andrews: Send them a telegram right away. Just say, 'Let 'em topple.'
clean: Send them a telegram right away. Just say, 'Let 'em topple.'

heading: Dialogue
raw: Zeke's Wife: Funny couple, ain't they?
clean: Funny couple, ain't they?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Zeke: Yeah.
clean: Yeah.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Zeke's Wife: If you ask me, I don't believe they're married.
clean: If you ask me, I don't believe they're married.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Zeke: They're married all right. I just seen the license.
clean: They're married all right. I just seen the license.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Zeke's Wife: They made me get them a rope and a blanket on a night like this. What do you reckon that's for?
clean: They made me get them a rope and a blanket on a night like this. What do you reckon that's for?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Zeke: Blamed if I know. I just brung 'em a trumpet.
clean: Blamed if I know. I just brung 'em a trumpet.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Zeke's Wife: A trumpet?
clean: A trumpet?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Zeke: Yeah, one of them toy things. They sent me to the store to get it.
clean: Yeah, one of them toy things. They sent me to the store to get it.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Zeke's Wife: But what in the world do they want a trumpet for?
clean: But what in the world do they want a trumpet for?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Zeke: Dunno.
clean: Dunno.
